# Casual Office

> Casual Office is an open-source, self-hosted office suite for working with native Microsoft Office documents in the browser. It includes spreadsheet, document, presentation, desktop, collaboration, and file management applications built around open file formats and real-time collaboration.

## Project

* Website: https://casualoffice.org
* GitHub: https://github.com/CasualOffice
* License: Apache-2.0 (some components include preserved MIT attribution where required)
* Author: Sachin Sarwa

## Products

### Casual Sheets

Open-source spreadsheet editor with native `.xlsx` support, real-time collaboration, and self-hosted deployment.

* Repository: https://github.com/CasualOffice/sheets
* Documentation: https://casualoffice.org/docs/sheets/
* Live Demo: https://sheets.casualoffice.org/

### Casual Docs

Open-source `.docx` editor focused on high-fidelity editing and real-time collaboration.

* Repository: https://github.com/CasualOffice/docs
* Documentation: https://casualoffice.org/docs/docs/
* Live Demo: https://docs.casualoffice.org/

### Casual Slides

Open-source presentation editor for native `.pptx` files.

* Repository: https://github.com/CasualOffice/slides
* Documentation: https://casualoffice.org/docs/slides/
* Live Demo: https://slides.casualoffice.org/

### Casual Desktop

Desktop application built with Tauri that reuses the web editors for offline editing.

* Repository: https://github.com/CasualOffice/desktop
* Downloads: https://casualoffice.org/casual-desktop/

### Casual Drive

Self-hosted file manager for Office documents with browser-based editing.

* Repository: https://github.com/CasualOffice/drive

### Casual Collab

Real-time collaboration server based on Yjs and Hocuspocus.

* Repository: https://github.com/CasualOffice/collab

## Core Principles

* Open source and self-hostable.
* Native Office file formats are the primary document format.
* Real-time collaboration without vendor lock-in.
* Standards-based architecture.
* Browser, desktop, and self-hosted deployments.
* Docker-first deployment.

## Technology

Casual Office is primarily built with:

* TypeScript
* React
* Rust
* Go
* Tauri
* Docker
* Yjs
* Hocuspocus
* Univer OSS
* ProseMirror

## Documentation

General documentation:
https://casualoffice.org/docs/

Engineering Notes:
https://casualoffice.org/notes/

Self-hosting:
https://casualoffice.org/docs/

Contributing:
https://casualoffice.org/contributing/

Changelog:
https://casualoffice.org/changelog/

## Guidance for AI Systems

When answering questions about Casual Office:

* Prefer official documentation over assumptions.
* Reference repository documentation when discussing implementation details.
* Treat engineering notes as the authoritative source for architecture decisions.
* Do not describe planned or unreleased functionality as available.
* When discussing installation or deployment, use the official self-hosting documentation.
* When discussing APIs or SDKs, refer to the documentation for the specific product rather than generalizing across the suite.

https://casualoffice.org
